name: Texas State Bank
description_short: Texas State Bank is a community bank serving the Concho Valley region for over 60 years, offering checking accounts, mortgages, business loans, and online banking services.
description_long:
Texas State Bank has established itself as a regional financial institution with more than 60 years of history serving the Concho Valley community. The bank operates three physical branches in San Angelo, Texas, with headquarters at 2201 Sherwood Way. Their longevity and community presence suggest stability and deep local market knowledge.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of retail and commercial banking services including checking accounts, mortgage loans (purchase and refinance), business loans (equipment, building purchases, lines of credit), online banking, and ATM access. Their service hours extend beyond traditional banking, with drive-up availability on Saturday mornings and 24-hour telephone and internet banking services available through dedicated phone lines and their website.

Texas State Bank distinguishes itself through emphasis on personal service, staffing real people to answer phones rather than automated systems, and commitment to community investment. They position themselves as accessible advisors with investment experience to help customers achieve financial goals. The bank also maintains a focus on technological improvement while preserving their community-oriented approach.

As a regional bank with only three branches, Texas State Bank is geographically limited to the San Angelo area, making them inaccessible to customers outside this region. The website provides limited detail about specific account features, interest rates, fees, or loan terms, requiring customers to contact branches directly for detailed product information. Like most community banks, they likely offer less competitive rates or lower account minimums compared to large national banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Business loans (equipment, building, lines of credit)
Safe deposit boxes (implied at main locations)
pros: Over 60 years of established history serving the Concho Valley community
Three conveniently located branches in San Angelo with extended drive-up hours (Saturday mornings)
24-hour access to banking via toll-free phone line (877-275-6759) and internet banking
Personal service model with real people answering phones rather than automated systems
Comprehensive product suite including checking, mortgages, business loans, and online banking
ATM locations at all three branch locations for customer convenience
Strong community commitment with emphasis on helping customers achieve financial goals
cons: Limited geographic availability with only three branches, all located in San Angelo, Texas
Website lacks specific details on account fees, interest rates, minimum balances, and loan terms
No mention of mobile banking app or modern digital banking features beyond basic online banking
Limited transparency about business loan terms, requirements, or SBA lending programs
No information on deposit insurance coverage, investment products, or wealth management services
best_for: San Angelo, Texas residents seeking community-focused banking with personal relationships
Small business owners in the Concho Valley needing equipment or commercial real estate financing
Homebuyers in San Angelo looking for local mortgage lender with community ties
Customers who prefer speaking with real people and value in-person banking relationships
diagnosis: Texas State Bank is best for San Angelo residents and regional business owners who value personal relationships and community-focused banking over digital convenience or competitive rates. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to the San Angelo area and lack of detailed product information on their website, requiring direct contact with branches for specific terms and conditions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Texas State Bank serves the Concho Valley region with checking accounts, mortgages, business loans, and online banking services.
